speaker: Narrator
text: Everything’s been packed into the car. Blanche takes her seat on the right, and in a vain attempt to hide from eyeline, Christina’s state is such that she’s slumped over the fish in the back seat.

speaker: Blanche
text: (I’d have to drive her all the way through the fucking city and into her shitty fucking neighborhood.)

speaker: Blanche
text: (Fuck.)

speaker: Blanche
text: Christina, do you really think you can take the metro home?

speaker: Christina
text: (Patronizing bitch, I’d rather be ravaged by wolves than spend another second with you-)

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina feels something coming up her throat and forces it down with a loud, audible gulp!

speaker: Christina
text: Thank you, it must be a big burden on you to drive me home... Blanche!

speaker: Christina
text: Especially with the cost of petrol these days and the traffic, oh, you’re practically a saint!

speaker: Blanche
text: My parents raised me to always be generous to those in need!

speaker: Christina
text: Oh!

speaker: Pasaya
text: Okay, ahem... my drones...!

speaker: Narrator
text: A sad, long grunt is forced out from Christina’s throat. From the corner of her eye, Blanche can see the distress in Christina’s body as she languidly reaches into her pocket and pulls out her phone!

speaker: Narrator
text: Weakly handling her phone while her head slumps over to meet the screen. Whether it’s the residual water from the river or her sweat, drops of something are falling on the image of Pasaya. Christina’s breathing intensifies whenever she sees her avatar's ample breasts shake!

speaker: Pasaya
text: I know the hive loves karaoke night...!

speaker: Pasaya
text: I love all of you so, so, so, soooooo much, especially of you in the drone tier who’ve floored me with the requests!

speaker: Pasaya
text: Gystf320, you!

speaker: Pasaya
text: Anybody who’s still fucking with the Zoids these days ...

speaker: Pasaya
text: ...I guess if it was like the fucking...

speaker: Pasaya
text: Chaterpillars, before I go, what animal would you fuck if it were a 30-foot-tall metal robot thing?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Quickly!

speaker: Pasaya
text: It’s almost 5 in the fucking morning hive time!

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina leaned closer to her phone. From the driver’s seat, all Blanche could see was a woman dangling over her seatbelt, breathing as if she had run a Marathon, broken up only by intense fits of coughing!

speaker: Blanche
text: (Was she going to die in my car?)

speaker: Blanche
text: Christina?

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina’s body ambles up like a zombie rising from its grave. Her face is covered in wetness. Drool from the mouth. Snot from the nose. Sweat from the forehead, the eyes that look back are puffy and red from irritation!

speaker: Blanche
text: Do you think... there must be... a better way to get back to your building?

speaker: Christina
text: Try-

speaker: Christina
text: Hack, hack!

speaker: Christina
text: Gasp, wheeze!

speaker: Christina
text: Oh god, pull over, pull over!

speaker: Blanche
text: Where you fucking...

speaker: Narrator
text: She couldn’t abandon her to the streets; her grade depended on her at least surviving long enough for her to hit send on the assignment and graduate. At least a C. She was certain that Christina would find some excuse for her to pay for an emergency room visit, coverage or not. So, for now, her best bet was to slow down and stop somewhere and close her eyes and ears!

speaker: Narrator
text: There was a patch of greenery, a park, with a trash can. A few kids are kicking a ball around with a little dog joining in the fun. She stops in the bike lane. Christina, holding her mouth in one hand, frantically pulls open the door of her sedan, sees a bush, and releases with a hearty ‘blech’.

speaker: Narrator
text: She turns around to look at Blanche with a drop or so still on her lip!

speaker: Christina
text: Oh. My bad!

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina brushes it off her face and leaves it on the bush. Slowly, she hobbles back to the car and sits down again, resting her head against the cooler filled with fish!

speaker: Blanche
text: So you feel better now, with all of that illness gone from your body?

speaker: Christina
text: Yes, yes, I think so!

speaker: Narrator
text: It’s not a long drive from the park to Christina's place, but it feels like they might as well be making the drive to Helsinki. Blanche notices quickly that the only reason Christina hasn’t erupted a second time is that virtually everything but her own stomach was released in the park. Hacks and gags, for about fifteen minutes before she can’t take it anymore!

speaker: Blanche
text: Christina, what kind of music do you listen to?

speaker: Christina
text: Hah!

speaker: Blanche
text: She’s fucking with me, or she’s already gone deaf with those damned earphones!

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche turns on the radio with force. It’s already dialed into the good metal station where a song about the Battle of Ain Jalut just at the point where the Egyptian Mamluks start beating the Mongols ass!

speaker: Blanche
text: My fucking shit!

speaker: Narrator
text: This goes on for an hour or so!
